title: Lines, Containers, Headings, Paragraphs, Lists, Quotes, and Fenced Code · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for lines, containers, headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, and fenced code and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for line-oriented block parser with explicit continuation rules. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Represent line content, indentation, blankness, offset, tab expansion, newline, and container prefix without discarding source positions.
Apply named block-start and continuation precedence so paragraphs, headings, quotes, lists, thematic breaks, and code resolve consistently.
Parse ATX headings and paragraphs with closing-marker, interruption, inline-content, and empty-heading evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Fenced Code, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Fenced Code precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Fenced Code, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 6196 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Fenced Code, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Any three backticks close every fenced code block.

stimulus.title: Fenced Code failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Any three backticks close every fenced code block.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fenced Code
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Fenced Code: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 7815 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to parse headings, paragraphs, containers, and fenced code with explicit line consumption; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fenced Code.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Fenced Code, keep Fenced Code connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Match opening and closing fence character and length, retain info text and literal content, and define end-of-file behavior.
